translated: Right?
Man, it really is a great story.
Ena is so loved.
I'm so grateful we got such a wonderful story.
With that said, I'd like to talk about the music after we watch the 3DMV that was written specifically for this event.
Please, take a look.
Breathing inside a borrowed life, in a body that can't become anyone.
If I dive deep into the depths, the me from that day...
is still smiling.
Looking back, there were certainly fragments of dreams sprouting in my chest.
Pressed down by the weight of strings, they eventually turned into hatred.
There's no use in trying to stroke the past.
This small, gentle light... my belief still won't reach.
Even so, I want to draw more of the future with these hands on the white scenery before me.
Even if the past isn't beautiful, I'll be myself.
I just want to draw...
this world that reflects in my eyes.
And we're back. Sorry about that.
My screen just popped up there.
That was the 3DMV for the song Chouchou-P wrote for this event, titled "The Name of Your Drawing."
Wait, I got mixed up. That was the 3DMV for "The Name of Your Drawing."
Uh...
Should I do this first? Oh, it's fine.
For a second, I thought my own notes were Chouchou-P's comments.
I was like, "Wait, what?"
But I was wrong.
Let me read the notes I sent to the staff beforehand.
Here they are.
"Just listening to it makes me feel Ena's journey, and it brings me to tears."
"The title contains her name, and I felt it was truly a song written for Ena."
"I sang it with her determination to turn regret and pain into strength, and with the positive feelings she found." That's what past-me emailed to the staff.
Chouchou-P's actual comments were introduced on the ProSeka News Station and such.
They should be on Twitter—I mean, X—as well, so please check them out.
But yeah, those were the feelings I put into the song.
Well...
There are lyrics that feel like she's looking back at the past.
But it's like the current Ena is the one doing the reflecting.
I'm sure Ena has regrets, for sure.
But I think she's a girl who understands that those regrets made her who she is today.
So even for the phrases about the past, I didn't want to sing them with the pure agony of the old Ena. I sang with my own sense of resolve.
Like I said during the story part, I tried to include that sense of relief and the brightness of finally understanding.
But this isn't the goal; it's a new start.
I was very conscious of capturing that middle-ground feeling while singing.
So rather than a huge explosion of emotion, it's more like quietly stirring up words within myself to cheer myself on.
Like patting my own chest and saying, "Let's do this."
I sang it with that image of self-encouragement in mind.
I just end up crying, though.
I've already heard the full version, you see.
Since I'm the one who sang it.
I'm sorry.
I just can't help but cry.
It's no good.
Both this story and the song just make me tear up.
What am I going to do? What should we do?
I thought I wouldn't cry this time, either during the talk or the story...
But I did anyway.
Yeah.
To those of you who felt a bit jealous that I've heard the full version, don't worry.
The 2DMV for this new song, "The Name of Your Drawing," will be uploaded to the official ProSeka YouTube channel at 10:15 PM.
Please check that out along with the 3DMV.
If it's like the previous 2DMVs, that means you'll be able to hear the full version too, right?
I haven't even seen the 2DMV myself yet.
Now, to give some belated thoughts on the 3DMV... I recorded the full song, so I knew how the parts were split.
I knew which parts would be Ena's.
The song starts with the other three members of Niigo in order.
So in the 3DMV inside my head—my imaginary 3DMV—it didn't start with Ena.
I was wondering how it would actually begin.
Would all four or five of them appear at once?
Or would it start with Mizuki, who has the first line? But then I saw Ena was on screen the whole time.
Of course, I'm always happy when other characters get a lot of screen time for their fans, too.
But this MV put such a huge spotlight on Ena, and it matched the story so perfectly. It really felt like it was capturing Ena's way of life.
As her voice actress, that made me so incredibly happy.
It's always the case, but the songs for Ena's stories always feel like the location is gradually moving closer to the surface.
Well, that's just my own geeky analysis.
But things like that make me happy too.
I've only seen it a few times myself, so I want to sit down and watch it properly.
I'm so excited for the 2DMV.
Yeah.
I think that's everything... I don't think I've left anything out.
Well, I've truly entrusted everything to Ena's voice.
